source_jobs.title: Product Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

At Hiverge, our mission is to build algorithmic superintelligence: an AI discovery engine that automatically writes algorithms for the most critical optimisation problems.

Our founding team is composed of world-class researchers from Google DeepMind and University of Cambridge, having co-developed AlphaTensor, FunSearch, and Nobel-prize winning AlphaFold. At Hiverge, you will be working with the brightest minds in AI, algorithms, and mathematics.

If you’re passionate about AI and eager to shape transformative technologies, we’d love to hear from you.

 

About this role

We're looking for a versatile Product Engineer to help build the platform through which researchers and engineers discover optimal algorithms. You'll work across the stack, contributing to architectural decisions and shaping how users define, run, and observe AI-driven experiments. This role combines hands-on engineering with the opportunity to influence product direction as we scale.


You will:

 - Own full-stack solutions end to end. You’ll design, build, and operate systems across the stack: from front-end user experience to low-latency backend infrastructure.

 - Collaborate closely with product, engineering, and customer teams to capture requirements and deliver production-ready solutions.

 - Build modular, maintainable UI components that feel seamless and fast.
   
    

Qualifications

 - 5+ years of experience working as a software developer.

 - Proven track record of shipping user-facing products end-to-end, from database to UI.

 - Product mindset: able to contribute to product design, understand user requirements, and prioritise features.

 - Production experience with TypeScript, React and at least one backend language (Python, Golang, Rust etc).

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, a related field, or equivalent experience.

 

What sets you apart

 - Familiarity with AI agents or LLM-based systems.

 - Familiarity with relational databases (PostgreSQL/MySQL) and modern cloud infrastructure practices (AWS/GCP/Azure, Kubernetes).

 - Experience with secure development practices and cloud security.

 - Experience contributing to open-source projects or maintaining community projects is highly valued.

 

We offer competitive compensation, generous benefits packages, and offer a stimulating work environment, with the opportunity to contribute to the future of generative AI.

source_jobs.title: Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

At Hiverge, our mission is to build algorithmic superintelligence: an AI discovery engine that automatically writes algorithms for the most critical optimisation problems.

Our founding team is composed of world-class researchers from Google DeepMind and University of Cambridge, having co-developed AlphaTensor, FunSearch, and Nobel-prize winning AlphaFold. At Hiverge, you will be working with the brightest minds in AI, algorithms, and mathematics.

If you’re passionate about AI and eager to shape transformative technologies, we’d love to hear from you.

 

About this role

We are seeking a skilled Platform Engineer to join our team. You will play a key role in designing, deploying, and maintaining our cloud infrastructure. Your work will directly impact the performance, reliability, security and cost-efficiency of our AI workloads.


You will:

 - Design, implement, and maintain cloud-based infrastructure on major cloud providers (AWS, GCP, Azure, etc).

 - Design and implement security best practices and infrastructure hardening for all cloud deployments.

 - Deploy and operate AI agents at scale, ensuring high availability and robust performance.

 - Implement monitoring, logging, and observability solutions to enhance system visibility.

 - Collaborate closely with product and engineering teams to align infrastructure with business needs.

 - Take ownership of meeting defined SLOs, including participating in on-call rotations.
   
    

Qualifications

 - 5+ years of experience working with cloud infrastructure in a major cloud provider (AWS, GCP, Azure).

 - Experience with infrastructure-as-code (IaC) in a production environment (Terraform, Helm etc).

 - Deep understanding of cloud security best practices.

 - Experience with Kubernetes.

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, a related field, or equivalent experience.

 

What sets you apart

 - Familiarity with security frameworks and compliance standards such as SOC 2 or ISO 27001.

 - Experience with large-scale AI / ML workloads (particularly using GPUs).

 - Familiarity with AI agents or LLM-based systems, especially LLM inference systems.

 - Experience with Golang and Python.

 - Open-source contributions or active community involvement is highly valued.

 

We offer competitive compensation, generous benefits packages, and offer a stimulating work environment, with the opportunity to contribute to the future of generative AI.

source_jobs.title: Research Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Who we are

At Hiverge, our mission is to build algorithmic superintelligence: an AI discovery engine that automatically writes algorithms for the most critical optimisation problems.

Our founding team is composed of world-class researchers from Google DeepMind and University of Cambridge, having co-developed AlphaTensor, FunSearch, AlphaEvolve and Nobel-prize winning AlphaFold. At Hiverge, you will be working with the brightest minds in AI, algorithms, and mathematics.

If you’re passionate about AI and eager to shape transformative technologies, we’d love to hear from you.

About this role

We are seeking a Research Engineer. You will be in charge of researching and implementing ideas around making agents collaborate together. You will play a key role in designing the next generation of discovery engines.

You will:

 - Investigate how research agents can work together to achieve a predetermined goal, and iterate quickly on these research ideas.

 - Design and run experiments to improve the capabilities and efficiency of our system.

 - Work together with our team of research engineers and leadership to align technical advances with product and business objectives.

 - Stay at the forefront of developments in AI agents, language models, search, optimisation, and automated discovery.

Qualifications

 - Having a PhD in Maths/CS/EE or related field, with a good track-record of publications.

 - Having contributed to/started open source projects, demonstrating strong software engineering skills with experience building complex systems in Python.

 - Excellent communication skills and the ability to collaborate across research and engineering disciplines.

What sets you apart

 - Experience with GPU programming / optimisation.

 - Experience with agentic systems, planning, or reasoning frameworks.

 - Background in algorithms & optimisation.

 - Experience building evaluation frameworks and experimentation platforms.

 - Strong problem-solving ability and comfort working in ambiguous, fast-moving environments.

 - Having experience working in a startup environment.

We offer competitive compensation, generous benefits packages, and offer a stimulating work environment, with the opportunity to contribute to the future of generative AI.

source_jobs.title: Research Engineer Intern
source_jobs.description:
WHO WE ARE

At Hiverge, our mission is to build algorithmic superintelligence: an AI discovery engine that automatically writes algorithms for the most critical optimisation problems.

Our founding team is composed of world-class researchers from Google DeepMind and University of Cambridge, having co-developed AlphaTensor, FunSearch, and Nobel-prize winning AlphaFold. At Hiverge, you will be working with the brightest minds in AI, algorithms, and mathematics.

If you’re passionate about AI and eager to shape transformative technologies, we’d love to hear from you.




ABOUT THIS ROLE

This role designs, trains, and evaluates LLM‑centric systems. The work blends implementation, experimentation, and benchmarking.

As a Research Engineer Intern you will:

 - Support the development of scalable experimentation foundations with strong reproducibility and observability

 - Help create and improve robust prompt and code-selection tooling.

 - Assist in optimizing our system’s inference and infrastructure for better efficiency.

 - Work with the team (ML engineers and researchers) to implement new ideas.

 - Engage with the research community to advance the state of algorithmic discovery.



QUALIFICATIONS

 - Masters degree in Computer Science, Engineering, related field, or equivalent experience.

 - Strong coding skills in Python and experience with PyTorch/JAX and modern LLM tooling for inference and fine‑tuning.

 - Experience in applied research engineering, demonstrated by impactful academic projects or internships in LLMs, search/RL, or compilers/verification.

 - Exposure to evaluation frameworks, unit/property‑based testing, and performance profiling.



WHAT SETS THE CANDIDATE APART

 - Contributions to open research or community tooling.

 - Familiarity with algorithm design, and practical understanding of reinforcement learning, evolutionary methods and optimization methods.


We offer competitive compensation and a stimulating work environment, with the opportunity to contribute to the future of generative AI.

Outstanding interns may be considered for a full-time Research Engineer position upon completion of the internship.

notes: AI-focused startup with strong research background
